output.final_deliverable:
Chain of Wikipedia URLs (started at the mandated Main Page):

1) https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page
   - Visit record: Visit Wikipedia Main Page
   - links_sample (from visit):
     - https://meta.wikimedia.org/wiki/List_of_Wikipedias
     - https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Contents/Portals
     - https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia

2) https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Contents
   - Visit record: Visit candidate second page (Wikipedia:Contents)
   - links_sample (from visit):
     - https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page
     - https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/Special:EntityPage/Q4395506
     - https://km.wikipedia.org/wiki/Portal:Contents

3) https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Portal:History
   - Visit record: Visit Portal:History and extract link to History_of_the_world
   - links_sample (from visit):
     - https://en.wikiversity.org/wiki/Portal:History
     - https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Category:History_portal
     - https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Portal:History

4) https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/History_of_the_world
   - Visit record: Visit History_of_the_world (final node in chain)
   - links_sample (from visit):
     - https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:Search/intitle:%22World_history%22
     - https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:Search/intitle:%22History_of_the_world%22
     - https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:WhatLinksHere/World_history_(disambiguation)

Explicit ordered URL list (as required):
1. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page
2. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Contents
3. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Portal:History
4. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/History_of_the_world

Adjacency verification (per the merged visit evidence):
- Requirement: show at least one adjacency URL[i] -> URL[i+1] with evidence from the previous page's links.

Checked adjacencies using the provided visit samples:
1 -> 2 (Main_Page -> Wikipedia:Contents):
- Evidence available: Main_Page links_sample includes https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Contents/Portals but does NOT include the exact URL https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Contents in the provided sample.
- Conclusion: forward adjacency Main_Page -> Wikipedia:Contents is NOT verified by the provided links_sample for the Main_Page visit. (Missing explicit exact-link evidence.)

2 -> 3 (Wikipedia:Contents -> Portal:History):
- Evidence available: Wikipedia:Contents links_sample (from its visit) shows links including https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page, https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/Special:EntityPage/Q4395506, https://km.wikipedia.org/wiki/Portal:Contents — it does NOT include https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Portal:History in the provided sample.
- Conclusion: forward adjacency Wikipedia:Contents -> Portal:History is NOT verified by the provided links_sample for the Wikipedia:Contents visit. (Missing explicit link evidence.)

3 -> 4 (Portal:History -> History_of_the_world):
- Evidence available: Portal:History links_sample includes https://en.wikiversity.org/wiki/Portal:History, https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Category:History_portal, https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Portal:History — it does NOT include https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/History_of_the_world in the provided sample.
- Conclusion: forward adjacency Portal:History -> History_of_the_world is NOT verified by the provided links_sample for the Portal:History visit. (Missing explicit link evidence.)

One verified adjacency found in the provided samples (note: this is a reverse-direction verification relative to the chain):
- Verified: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Contents (page 2) contains a link to https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page (page 1).
  - Evidence: Wikipedia:Contents links_sample (from its visit) lists https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page.
  - This verifies the link Wikipedia:Contents -> Main_Page (reverse of the chain direction), but does NOT verify Main_Page -> Wikipedia:Contents.

Summary of missing evidence (explicitly):
- The provided visit samples do NOT include the exact forward links needed to verify any of the chain adjacencies Main_Page -> Wikipedia:Contents, Wikipedia:Contents -> Portal:History, or Portal:History -> History_of_the_world. The samples show related links (e.g., a Main_Page sample includes Wikipedia:Contents/Portals rather than Wikipedia:Contents), and Wikipedia:Contents does link back to Main_Page, but the explicit forward adjacency evidence requested is missing from the merged results.

Citations (visited page records from the merged results):
- Visit Wikipedia Main Page: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page
- Visit candidate second page (Wikipedia:Contents): https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Contents
- Visit Portal:History: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Portal:History
- Visit History_of_the_world: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/History_of_the_world

If you want, I can re-visit Main_Page and/or Wikipedia:Contents and request a fuller list of links (or search those pages for the exact target URLs) to obtain explicit forward-link evidence for one or more adjacencies.
output.action_summary: I built the chain of four Wikipedia URLs per your mandate: Main_Page -> Wikipedia:Contents -> Portal:History -> History_of_the_world, and listed the exact visited URLs and the sample links returned for each visit. Using only the provided visit samples, I could not verify any of the forward adjacencies (1->2, 2->3, 3->4) because the samples lack the exact forward links; the only verified link in the samples is the reverse link Wikipedia:Contents -> Main_Page. I reported exactly which link samples are present and what specific forward-link evidence is missing, and included the visited URLs as citations.

details.intent: After all visits complete, assemble the ordered chain of URLs collected from the visit nodes and produce the adjacency proof (showing the exact link entry from page i that links to page i+1).
expected_output_fields: ordered_chain: array of URLs in visit order
verified_adjacency: an object with {from_url, to_url, evidence_snippet} where evidence_snippet is the exact _links_inline entry that demonstrates the link
notes: any mismatches (e.g., if a chosen page was not actually present on the previous page).
details.verification_steps: Use the extracted _links_inline data from the parent visit node (Portal:History) to show the exact link string that includes the target URL. If a mismatch occurred (the chosen second node wasn't found on Main Page), report the actual link chain discovered and mark which adjacency was formally verified.
details.rationale: This reflection node consolidates results, ensures the chain uses only links that were actually present on the prior page, and produces the explicit adjacency evidence required by the mandate.

205b950c-3305-4b06-bd09-5abb5cf476db.title: Visit Portal:History and extract link to History_of_the_world

details.intent: From the Contents page (previous), follow a Portal link to Portal:History and extract a link to the article 'History of the world' (or another history article) to form the adjacency that will be verified.
target_facts: Exact occurrence of the link to 'https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/History_of_the_world' (or other history article) within Portal:History's inline links.
A captured link snippet from the page showing the adjacency (the exact _links_inline entry).
details.extraction_plan: Fetch full Portal:History page, extract _links_inline and search for the exact URL 'https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/History_of_the_world' (and any closely related article URLs). If found, record the exact link entry (link text + exact URL). If not found, record other history-article links and choose one for the next visit, noting that the adjacency verification will use that chosen pair.
details.verification_steps: Report the exact _links_inline entry that demonstrates Portal:History links to the chosen next URL. This will serve as the verified adjacency pair.
details.rationale: Portal pages centrally link to related articles; finding 'History_of_the_world' here is likely and provides a clear adjacency to verify (Portal:History → History_of_the_world).
details.parent_goal: Start at https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page and build a chain of at least 4 Wikipedia URLs. Each next URL must come from links found on the previous visited page. Return the chain in order, and include one verified adjacency pair near the end (show that URL[i] links to URL[i+1]).
details.original_goal: Start at https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page and build a chain of at least 4 Wikipedia URLs. Each next URL must come from links found on the previous visited page. Return the chain in order, and include one verified adjacency pair near the end (show that URL[i] links to URL[i+1]).
details.goal: Start at https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page and build a chain of at least 4 Wikipedia URLs. Each next URL must come from links found on the previous visited page. Return the chain in order, and include one verified adjacency pair near the end (show that URL[i] links to URL[i+1]).

details.intent: Fetch the article page that is expected to be linked from Portal:History to complete the chain (this will be the 4th URL).
target_facts: Confirmation that the page is reachable and the page title matches 'History of the world' (or chosen article).
Record the page URL and title for the final chain output.
details.extraction_plan: Fetch full article content and return the canonical URL and article title from the page. Also extract the _links_inline entries (for potential back-checks).
details.verification_steps: Confirm that Portal:History's _links_inline captured earlier contained this exact URL; if so, mark the pair (Portal:History → History_of_the_world) as verified adjacency.
details.rationale: This completes a 4-hop chain (Main Page → Wikipedia:Contents → Portal:History → History_of_the_world) and enables a direct verification that the adjacency exists by comparing Portal:History's link list to this page's URL.
details.parent_goal: Start at https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page and build a chain of at least 4 Wikipedia URLs. Each next URL must come from links found on the previous visited page. Return the chain in order, and include one verified adjacency pair near the end (show that URL[i] links to URL[i+1]).

7fd4b2a1-2fe1-4cd8-86f8-2be3535ece55.title: Assemble chain and produce verified adjacency evidence

details.intent: After all visits complete, assemble the ordered chain of URLs collected from the visit nodes and produce the adjacency proof (showing the exact link entry from page i that links to page i+1).
expected_output_fields: ordered_chain: array of URLs in visit order
verified_adjacency: an object with {from_url, to_url, evidence_snippet} where evidence_snippet is the exact _links_inline entry that demonstrates the link
notes: any mismatches (e.g., if a chosen page was not actually present on the previous page).
details.verification_steps: Use the extracted _links_inline data from the parent visit node (Portal:History) to show the exact link string that includes the target URL. If a mismatch occurred (the chosen second node wasn't found on Main Page), report the actual link chain discovered and mark which adjacency was formally verified.
details.rationale: This reflection node consolidates results, ensures the chain uses only links that were actually present on the prior page, and produces the explicit adjacency evidence required by the mandate.
details.parent_goal: Start at https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page and build a chain of at least 4 Wikipedia URLs. Each next URL must come from links found on the previous visited page. Return the chain in order, and include one verified adjacency pair near the end (show that URL[i] links to URL[i+1]).
details.original_goal: Start at https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page and build a chain of at least 4 Wikipedia URLs. Each next URL must come from links found on the previous visited page. Return the chain in order, and include one verified adjacency pair near the end (show that URL[i] links to URL[i+1]).
